NEXUS V4: Clean slate — code only, no fat

6.6GB → 40MB. Stripped all data, training artifacts, archives,
debug images, temp scripts, node_modules, and build outputs.

Sanitized: wallet private key removed from polygon_config.json.
Removed: pi_servers (BROK/SNARF retired), nexus-os, 3d_models,
training data, installer builds, archive zips, 50+ debug JPGs,
40+ tmp_*.py scripts, duplicate marketplace dir.

Kept: nexus_v2 (main app), src (APIs + Danielson scanner),
workers (Cloudflare), firmware (ESP32), blockchain (contracts),
modules (scrapers), docs, patent, legal, tools, assets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 159d: Phase 1 cleanup + Phase 3 goldmine adapter enrichments

Phase 1 (executed): Prior-season cleanup reduced gap from 57.4% to 47.5%
- 95 prior-season leagues archived (21,334 standings teams)
- 11,226 prior-season staging rows marked processed
- Created s159_phase1_clean.cjs for reproducible execution

Phase 3: Goldmine team ID extraction for 2 adapters
- MLSNext: Added get_filters API call to build team name→ID registry
  per age group. Fallback chain: image URL > dropdown > API registry.
- HTGSports: Added REST API /api/standings/{eventId} call for team
  name→ID registry. Fallback chain: DOM dropdown > REST API.
- SportsAffinity, PlayMetrics, OttoSport: Already have team ID
  extraction from Session 156 (needs re-scrape to populate).
- Coast Soccer: No team IDs available in HTML platform.

Also added docs/s159_goldmine_integration_plan.md — master plan for
driving standings-only gap to 0%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Apply guidelines audit fixes and update docs

- Accessibility: add .accessibilityHidden(true) to history icon in TaskDetailView
- Error handling: introduce HouseholdError: LocalizedError (fetchFailed/saveFailed);
  TaskStore.error is now HouseholdError? instead of bare Error?
- Naming: rename isSkipEntry → isSkipped across CompletionEntry+Extensions,
  TaskStore, TaskDetailView, HistoryView
- Naming: replace single-letter locals (t, g, ctx, i) with descriptive names
  in GroupListView, Persistence, TaskListView
- EditTaskView: use frequency.displayLabel(multiplier:) instead of hand-rolled label
- Docs: update README, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md with skip/undo features,
  lastScheduledAt, isSkip schema additions, new model files, HouseholdError

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct state-reset order so loading indicator actually renders

ticketStore.setError() always writes isLoading:false in its reducer.
Calling setLoading(true) then setError(null) left React 18's automatic
batching with a net isLoading:false before the first await, so the
spinner and button loading text were never displayed.

Swap to setError(null) → setLoading(true) so isLoading:true survives
the batch and React renders the loading state before the API call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disambiguate PostgREST join on admin_user_roles → profiles

admin_user_roles has two FKs to profiles (user_id and assigned_by),
causing PostgREST to fail with an ambiguous relationship error.

commit.message:
fix: use pnpm config for scoped registry instead of unsupported CLI flag

pnpm does not support scoped registry flags as CLI arguments. Instead,
run `pnpm config set` to configure the @roylet-gs scoped registry in
global .npmrc before install, preventing better-sqlite3 404 errors.

commit.message:
handover: 2026-03-05 h2見出し装飾追加

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Proxy external APIs through Worker, simplify env config (#37)

* fix(live-window): use IP registry location instead of weather API name

Stop fetchWeather from overwriting location.name with the OpenWeather
API response — preserve the city name set by IP Registry. Bump cache
version to clear stale entries.

Also fix file-loader triple-reload on startup by skipping the
.generated/links.yaml write when content is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): proxy IP geolocation through Cloudflare Worker

Move the IP Registry geolocation call from the client to a server-side
proxy endpoint (`GET /location`) in the Cloudflare Worker. This removes
the API key from client-side code and enables future provider switching
with zero client changes.

- Add `/location` endpoint that forwards user IP via CF-Connecting-IP
- Skip private IPs (localhost, 10.x, etc.) for local dev compatibility
- Replace `ipregistry-key` attribute with `api-url` on live-window
- Replace `PUBLIC_IPREGISTRY_KEY` env var with `PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`
- Update CI/CD, tests, README, and config files

commit.message:
Add Claude-powered workout and meal plan generation

- New generate-workout-plan edge function: builds personalized 12-week
  progressive programs using Claude Sonnet based on user intake data.
  Supports per-phase generation to avoid timeout. Includes Dom's
  non-negotiables: mobility in every workout, push-ups always,
  burpees almost always, bodyweight king even with full gym.
  Detailed form breakdowns, breathing, common mistakes, scaling.

- New generate-meal-plan edge function: builds personalized 7-day
  meal plans with full recipes, macros, grocery lists, meal prep
  instructions, and swap options. Respects dietary restrictions.

- IntakeComplete now triggers plan generation on intake completion
  for Gen Pop and Free World tiers (including admin test flow).

- Migration adds user_id, grocery_list, meal_prep_instructions
  columns to meal_plan_templates for per-user plans.

commit.message:
Fix duplicate code block in renderer.py causing double render time

Removed duplicate image extraction and saving code block (lines 304-335)
that was causing each object to be rendered twice. This bug was causing
DLC jobs to take 2x expected time.

Changes:
- Deleted 33 lines of duplicate code in render_thumbnail_wo_bg()
- Reorganized imports (std lib first, then third party)
- Moved traceback import to module level (was imported inline twice)

Fixes: Double render time issue in render_thumbnail_wo_bg method

commit.message:
Feature: Dashboard de estadísticas de inscripciones

Backend (server.js):
- Agregar 9 endpoints para estadísticas de inscripciones
- /api/stats-inscripciones/totales - Totales generales
- /api/stats-inscripciones/por-sede - Por sede
- /api/stats-inscripciones/por-area - Por área
- /api/stats-inscripciones/por-turno - Por turno
- /api/stats-inscripciones/por-dia - Por día de semana
- /api/stats-inscripciones/pagos-por-dia - Total pagos ≥S/200
- /api/stats-inscripciones/filtro-completo - Filtros combinados con LEFT JOIN
- /api/stats-inscripciones/todas-sedes - Todas las sedes
- /api/stats-inscripciones/opciones-por-sede/:id - Áreas y turnos por sede
- Filtros basados en grupo_aulas para mostrar 0 cuando no hay inscritos

Frontend (stats/index.html):
- Dashboard responsive con Tailwind CDN y Chart.js
- Cards: Total inscritos + 3 áreas (Biomédicas, Ingenierías, Sociales)
- Layout horizontal compacto para cards de áreas en móvil
- Gráfico de pastel para inscritos por sede (dinámico)
- Gráficos de barras para área, turno, día de semana
- Total de pagos desde 25 Feb 2026 (≥S/200)
- Filtros en cascada: Sede → Área y Turno disponibles
- Todas las sedes disponibles en filtro (incluidas sin inscritos)
- Resultados de filtro debajo de filtros de búsqueda
- Tabla con abreviaciones en móvil (BIO, ING, SOC)
- Botones responsive apilados en móvil
- Scroll optimizado a resultados de filtro
- Auto-refresh cada 5 minutos

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: whatsapp-mcp build failure — disable allowImportingTsExtensions

Root tsconfig sets allowImportingTsExtensions: true (requires noEmit),
but whatsapp-mcp needs noEmit: false to emit declarations. Explicitly
disable allowImportingTsExtensions in the package tsconfig to resolve
the conflict.

commit.message:
fix(triage): add llm config to simili.yaml and relax validation (#96)

Root cause: triage was broken in the main repo because .github/simili.yaml
had no `llm:` section. Config validation required llm.api_key, so the entire
config was discarded and replaced with an empty struct — zeroing out
qdrant.collection and causing Qdrant to reject the empty collection name.

Two-part fix:
- .github/simili.yaml: add explicit `llm:` section reusing GEMINI_API_KEY
- config.Validate(): remove llm.api_key from required fields, since the
  process command already falls back to embedding.api_key when it is unset.
  Strict validation was silently discarding a valid config instead of
  gracefully degrading.

Signed-off-by: Kavirubc <hapuarachchikaviru@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
